questions.question: The phrase “valour’s minion” used to describe Macbeth in the opening paragraph means he is
options: a servant who merely follows orders
a darling or favourite of courage
a soldier who despises war
a minion of the king’s court

questions.question: The term “Bellona’s bridegroom” suggests Macbeth was
options: married to a woman named Bellona
like a husband to the Roman goddess of war, implying his martial prowess
a peace-loving nobleman
about to marry a foreign princess

questions.question: The three witches are described as having “beards” and “withered skin”. This description serves to
options: make them appear comical and harmless
emphasise their unnatural and eerie nature
show they are ordinary old women
indicate they are messengers of the king

questions.question: When the first witch hails Macbeth as “Thane of Glamis”, Macbeth is “scarcely startled” because
options: he already knew the witches would greet him
he was already expecting to inherit that title
the title was already his by right, so the greeting was not surprising
he thought the witches were joking

questions.question: Macbeth has “no pretensions” to the title of Thane of Cawdor at the time of the witches’ greeting. This means he
options: did not desire any title
believed he had no claim to it and did not expect it
had refused the title earlier
thought Banquo deserved it more

questions.question: Banquo asks the witches why they “kindled hope in the heart of his dearest friend” but had little to say to him. This shows Banquo’s
options: jealousy and resentment towards Macbeth
curiosity and a desire to understand his own fate
anger at being ignored
intention to report the witches to the king

questions.question: The witches tell Banquo he shall be “lesser than Macbeth and greater, not so happy, but much happier.” This seeming paradox means
options: Banquo will be a happier man personally, though he will never be king
Banquo will become king after Macbeth dies
Banquo will suffer more than Macbeth
Banquo will be richer but less powerful

questions.question: The immediate event that “miraculously” corresponded with the witches’ prediction was
options: the king’s arrival at Inverness
the conferral of the title of Thane of Cawdor upon Macbeth
the death of the previous Thane of Cawdor
the arrival of Ross and Angus with a message of surrender

questions.question: After receiving the news of his new title, Macbeth says to Banquo, “Do you not hope that your children shall be the kings?” This indicates that
options: Macbeth is already contemplating the third prophecy seriously
he is merely making a light-hearted joke
he has already decided to murder Duncan
he wants to share the throne with Banquo’s family

questions.question: Banquo warns that the witches might be “ministers of darkness” who tell small truths to betray them into “deeds of greatest consequence.” This warning reveals Banquo’s
options: ambition to seize power himself
moral clarity and suspicion of supernatural temptation
desire to discourage Macbeth so he can claim the throne
plan to inform Duncan about the prophecy

questions.question: The extract states that Lady Macbeth “spurred on the reluctant purpose of Macbeth who felt compunction at the thoughts of blood.” The word “compunction” means
options: excitement
a feeling of guilt or moral unease
violent anger
indifference

questions.question: Lady Macbeth is described as having developed “the art of camouflaging her treacherous purposes with smiles and charming etiquette.” This foreshadows
options: her later descent into madness and sleepwalking
the ability to deceive Duncan and his retinue
her skill as a warrior
her genuine affection for Duncan

questions.question: The extract mentions that when Lady Macbeth approached the sleeping king, she saw something in his face that “resembled her own father.” This detail
options: shows her complete inhumanity
provides a moment of hesitation, revealing a remnant of filial feeling
proves she had no real intention to murder
indicates Duncan was her actual father

questions.question: Macbeth’s soliloquy on the reasons against the murder includes the point that he was the king’s “host”, so his duty should be to
options: protect the king, not bear the dagger himself
entertain the king, not disturb his sleep
send the king away for safety
inform the guards immediately

questions.question: In addition to being his host, Macbeth acknowledges Duncan as a king who was “clear of offence to his subjects” and “loving to his nobility.” This shows that Macbeth
options: hated the king
recognised Duncan’s just and merciful rule, which made the murder a graver sin
thought the king was weak and deserved to die
was lying to himself

questions.question: When Macbeth hesitates to proceed with the murder, Lady Macbeth accuses him of “cowardice and fickleness”. Her method of persuasion primarily relies on
options: physical threats
questioning his manhood and resolve
promising him wealth
appealing to his religious faith

questions.question: Lady Macbeth reveals the practicality of her plan by saying they will “lay the guilt of the deed on the drunken grooms.” This suggests she has
options: already drugged the grooms herself
considered the details of the murder beforehand
left everything to chance
no real intention of going through with the plot

questions.question: Before entering Duncan’s chamber, Macbeth sees a dagger hanging in the air, with drops of blood on its tip and blade. He tries to grasp it but it fades. This dagger is
options: a real weapon left by Lady Macbeth
a hallucination created by his “hot and oppressed brain”
a trick played by the grooms
a ghost sent by the witches

questions.question: Immediately after the murder, one groom laughs in his sleep and cries “Murder!” and the other says “God bless us!” and “Amen”. Macbeth’s inability to say “Amen” suggests
options: he had lost his voice from shouting
he felt cut off from divine grace and forgiveness
he did not hear the grooms properly
the grooms were mocking him

questions.question: The voice that Macbeth hears crying, “Sleep no more; Macbeth murders sleep” indicates that
options: someone is really calling out from outside the castle
Macbeth will literally never sleep again
Macbeth’s guilty conscience has destroyed his inner peace and ability to rest
Lady Macbeth is playing a trick on him

questions.question: After Macbeth returns with the daggers, Lady Macbeth “sent him to wash his hands of the blood” and herself stained the grooms’ cheeks. This shows Lady Macbeth is, at this moment,
options: more shaken than Macbeth
more practical and resolute in covering up the crime
remorseful
planning to confess

questions.question: Malcolm fled to the English court and Donalbain to Ireland after the murder. Their flight
options: proved their guilt in the murder
was a plan to raise an army immediately
made them appear suspicious, helping Macbeth’s claim
was ordered by Macbeth

questions.question: The extract says that after the murder, “the entire suspicion fell upon Macbeth.” However, it also says evidence was “sufficiently strong against the grooms”. What does this contradiction indicate?
options: The author made a mistake
Public opinion was split, but ultimately Macbeth was suspected despite the planted evidence
Macbeth confessed openly
Lady Macbeth betrayed her husband

questions.question: Macbeth and his queen could not forget the prophecy that “the children of Banquo… would be the kings after him.” This caused them to feel that they had “defiled their hands with blood” only to
options: make Banquo king
place Banquo’s posterity on the throne
earn eternal damnation with no benefit
secure Duncan’s sons’ inheritance

questions.question: At the banquet, Macbeth sees the ghost of Banquo sitting in his chair. None of the guests see it. This shows that
options: the ghost is an illusion conjured by the witches to frighten the guests
it is a product of Macbeth’s guilt-ridden mind
Banquo is actually alive and playing a trick
the guests are part of a conspiracy

questions.question: Lady Macbeth dismisses the guests by telling them that Macbeth’s fit is a “disorder he is often troubled with.” This lie is intended to
options: protect their secret and explain his strange behaviour
announce that Macbeth is unfit to be king
draw sympathy from the nobles
make the guests believe Banquo is really a ghost

questions.question: Fleance’s escape troubles Macbeth and his wife not only because he is a threat but because they now look upon him as
options: the rightful Thane of Cawdor
the father to a line of kings who would keep Banquo’s posterity on the throne
a spy for Malcolm
a future ally of Macduff

questions.question: The witches’ cauldron includes ingredients like “the liver of a Jew” and “the finger of a dead child.” These details serve to
options: show the witches’ cooking skills
highlight the grotesque and unnatural nature of the dark magic
suggest the witches are healers
make the scene comical

questions.question: The first apparition, an “armed head”, warns Macbeth to “Beware of the Thane of Fife.” The Thane of Fife is
options: Banquo
Macduff
Malcolm
Ross

questions.question: The second apparition, a “bloody child”, tells Macbeth to “laugh and scorn at the power of man born naturally of a woman’s womb.” This prophecy gives Macbeth the false idea that
options: no human can ever kill him
he is immortal against all men, because all men are born of women
only a child can defeat him
he should surrender to Macduff

questions.question: After hearing the second prophecy, Macbeth says he will “make assurance doubly sure” by killing Macduff. This reveals Macbeth’s
options: mercy and restraint
growing tyranny and determination to crush all potential threats
desire to follow the witches’ advice not to kill
plan to capture Macduff alive

questions.question: The third apparition is “a crowned child with a tree in his hand” and says Macbeth will never be vanquished until Birnam wood comes to Dunsinane. The “crowned child” likely symbolises
options: Fleance
Malcolm, the rightful future king
Banquo’s ghost
Macduff’s son

questions.question: Macbeth interprets the moving of Birnam wood as impossible because he thinks no one can “unfix the forest and move it from its earth-bound roots.” He therefore assumes he will
options: live the usual period of man’s life and not die violently
conquer England
be killed by a supernatural force
abdicate the throne peacefully

questions.question: After the three apparitions, a show of eight kings passes, the last one being Banquo with a glass in his hand. Banquo “smiled at Macbeth” and pointed to the images. This vision
options: reassures Macbeth of his lineage
horrifies him by confirming that Banquo’s descendants will rule
shows Banquo offering Macbeth the crown
is a dream from which Macbeth wakes up laughing

questions.question: The first news Macbeth receives after leaving the witches is that Macduff has fled to England. Stung with rage, Macbeth
options: orders Macduff’s capture but spares his family
brutally slaughters Macduff’s wife and children left in Scotland
sends a messenger for peace
kills himself

questions.question: As a result of Macbeth’s “merciless activities”, the extract notes that his nobility
options: became more loyal
gradually alienated, many fleeing to join Malcolm and Macduff
crowned him emperor
threw a grand banquet in his honour

questions.question: Macbeth begins to envy the dead Duncan because Duncan
options: had more money
now sleeps soundly in his grave, beyond the reach of treason, poison, domestic malice or foreign levies
was killed more honourably
will be avenged by angels

questions.question: The extract states that Lady Macbeth “ultimately died” because she could not bear the remorse of guilt and began to suffer from “somnambulism.” The term “somnambulism” means
options: violent fits
sleepwalking
hallucinations of ghosts
fever

questions.question: After the queen’s death, Macbeth is described as “careless of life and longed for death.” This is because he is
options: ill with an incurable disease
alone, burdened with guilt, and sees life as meaningless
planning to surrender to Malcolm
hiding in Birnam wood

questions.question: Despite his despair, Macbeth’s “ancient courage” is roused by the approaching army, and he determines to “die with armour on his back.” This means he chooses to
options: commit suicide
fight and die as a warrior rather than surrender
hide in the castle
flee to Ireland

questions.question: The messenger who reports the moving wood is pale and trembling. Macbeth first threatens to hang him alive on a tree till famine ends him, calling him “Liar”. This shows Macbeth’s
options: sense of humour
reliance on the literal interpretation of the prophecy and his violent reaction to doubt
kind treatment of messengers
complete trust in the messenger

questions.question: The moving wood is actually
options: an illusion caused by the witches
soldiers commanded by Malcolm carrying boughs from Birnam wood to conceal their numbers
a storm that uprooted trees and pushed them towards the castle
a herd of animals moving through the forest

questions.question: When Macduff confronts Macbeth, he calls him a “tyrant, murderer, hell-hound and a villain” specifically for
options: killing Duncan
slaughtering Macduff’s wife and innocent children
allying with the witches
burning Dunsinane

questions.question: Macbeth tells Macduff, “I bear a charmed life which must not yield to a man born naturally of a woman.” He says this because he
options: has a magical shield
completely trusts the literal meaning of the second apparition’s words
wants to frighten Macduff with a bluff
has drunk a potion

questions.question: Macduff declares he was “untimely taken out from his mother’s womb.” This revelation means Macduff was
options: born prematurely or via caesarean section, not in the ordinary manner of natural birth
a supernatural being
born years after his mother died
adopted

questions.question: On hearing Macduff’s birth secret, Macbeth laments that one should never trust “the lying equivocations of witches and juggling spirits, who deceive us in words that have double meanings.” This realisation marks Macbeth’s
options: final acceptance of the truth of the prophecies’ dual interpretation
immediate surrender to Macduff
decision to kill himself before Macduff can strike
plan to escape

questions.question: Macduff threatens to imprison Macbeth and exhibit him as a “monster” for the laity. Macbeth responds with “I will not live to kiss the ground before young Malcolm’s feet.” This shows Macbeth’s
options: pride and refusal to be humiliated, even in defeat
sudden love for Malcolm
desire to surrender with dignity
plan to beg for mercy later

questions.question: Macbeth’s final action is described as “his valiance returned with despair and he threw upon Macduff.” This implies that
options: he cowardly tried to run
in a last surge of desperate courage, he attacked Macduff and fought to the death
he fell on his own sword
he asked Macduff to join him

questions.question: Macduff severs Macbeth’s head and presents it to “the young and lawful new king”, who is
options: Donalbain
Fleance
Malcolm
Banquo’s ghost

questions.question: Overall, the extract presents Macbeth's tragedy as a consequence of
options: fate alone, with no personal responsibility
unchecked ambition, moral downfall, and misplaced trust in deceptive prophecies
Lady Macbeth’s sole villainy
the witches’ ability to control his actions completely
